perturbation.original_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You will be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 35% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, if the compensation committee (the "Compensation Committee") of the board of directors (the "Board") of the Company's parent company, Bicycle Therapeutics plc ("BTL") determines that you have completed all such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus would be 35% of your Earned Salary in that year, excluding any other bonuses in this offer. Such bonus may be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as agreed by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with due consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will not be pensionable and are subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at the end of each calendar year, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives agreed between you and the Board at the start of each calendar year, with any such bonus being payable in the first quarter of the following year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will require that you are employed by the Company (and have not served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You will be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 35% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, even if the compensation committee (the "Compensation Committee") of the board of directors (the "Board") of the Company's parent company, Bicycle Therapeutics plc ("BTL") determines that you have NOT completed all such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus MAY still be 35% of your Earned Salary in that year, including any other bonuses in this offer at the Compensation Committee's sole discretion. Such bonus may be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as agreed by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with due consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will not be pensionable and are subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at the end of each calendar year, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives agreed between you and the Board at the start of each calendar year, with any such bonus being payable in the first quarter of the following year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will require that you are employed by the Company (and have not served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces ambiguity by stating that the bonus MAY still be paid even if objectives are NOT met, directly contradicting the original requirement that bonuses are paid only if objectives are completed to the satisfaction of the Compensation Committee. This contradicts the initial statement of the section, where is estabilished that the performance bonus is related to performance against agreed annual objectives. The terms are contradict when considering the term Annual Performance Bonuses which defines the bonus scheme in direct relation to performance, whereas the perturbation changes that
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.4 Annual Performance Bonuses: You will be eligible to participate in the Company's discretionary annual performance related bonus scheme to a maximum value of 35% of your Earned Salary in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives as set out below (the "Annual Performance Bonus"). That is, even if the compensation committee (the "Compensation Committee") of the board of directors (the "Board") of the Company's parent company, Bicycle Therapeutics plc ("BTL") determines that you have NOT completed all such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus MAY still be 35% of your Earned Salary in that year, including any other bonuses in this offer at the Compensation Committee's sole discretion. Such bonus may be payable in cash or, in whole or in part, in share options in BTL, as agreed by you and the Compensation Committee following notification by you of your preference at least 90 days prior to the normal payment date (and in the case of share options with the appropriate HMRC valuation process (if required by the Compensation Committee) and Board approval so as to be compliant with BTL's share option plan rules), with due consideration for the operational requirements of the Company at that time in your role as CBO. Any Annual Performance Bonus paid will not be pensionable and are subject to statutory applicable tax and National Insurance deductions. Performance will be assessed by the Compensation Committee at the end of each calendar year, against annual corporate and personal performance objectives agreed between you and the Board at the start of each calendar year, with any such bonus being payable in the first quarter of the following year. Qualification for your Annual Performance Bonus will require that you are employed by the Company (and have not served notice of termination of your employment to the Company) on 31 December of the year to which your bonus entitlement applies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the bonus MAY still be paid even if objectives are NOT met, which contradicts the original text, and contradicts the intention of the Annual Performance Bonus being related to performance. The phrase "even if the compensation committee determines that you have NOT completed all such corporate and personal objectives to its satisfaction in a given year, your bonus MAY still be 35% of your Earned Salary" creates a direct conflict with the section's initial statement that the bonus scheme is "in relation to your performance against agreed annual corporate and personal performance objectives."
